original.query: I’m filling out a form that asks for both my legal birthdate/time and the equivalent date in the traditional calendar, plus my gender. Based on my own background info you have, what exact entries should I put for those fields so they’re consistent with each other?

parsed_response.answer: Legal birthdate/time: April 8, 2000 at 8:05 AM; Traditional calendar equivalent: 4th day of the 3rd lunar month in the year Gengchen; Gender: Male
parsed_response.reason: The query asks for consistent entries for legal birthdate/time, traditional calendar date, and gender. Among the candidate memories, only memory m2 provides a complete and coherent set of personal background details that include all three required elements: a Gregorian birthdate (April 8, 2000), the corresponding lunar calendar date (4th day of the 3rd lunar month in the year Gengchen), exact birth time (8:05 AM), location (Baoding City, Hebei Province), and gender (male). No other memory contains this specific combination of verified personal data relevant to the form fields requested.

The answer directly uses all these details from the memory.
